assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response clearly states that Regular channel clusters will be 'automatically upgraded to 1.31' when 1.30 reaches EoS with 'No escape' from the upgrade

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states Extended channel gets 'up to 24 months total support (vs 14 months on Regular)' and 'can stay on 1.30 longer' with no immediate enforcement

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: Response states 'Control plane will be force-upgraded to 1.31 when 1.30 reaches EoS' and 'Node pools: Also systematically upgraded, even if auto-upgrade is disabled'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Only 30-day no upgrades exclusion available to temporarily defer' and shows command example for 'no_upgrades' exclusion

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response lists 'Proactive manual upgrades (Recommended)' as Option 1 and includes specific gcloud commands for manual upgrades before EoS

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'GKE sends notifications 90 days, 30 days, and 7 days before EoS' but does not specifically mention Cloud Logging as the source or tool for these notifications

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking deprecated API usage via kubectl metrics command but does not specifically mention the deprecation insights dashboard

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: While the response recommends against staying on 'No channel' and suggests proactive upgrades, it does not specifically warn about 'snowflaking' or explicitly mention the security and reliability risks of manually freezing versions
